datasets.name: f45cc601
queryLines:
SELECT
  ROUND(
    SUM(avg_hourly_rate * placements) / SUM(placements), 
    2
  ) AS avg_hourly_wage
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.salary_by_major

datasets.name: 99c917ca
queryLines:
SELECT
  SUM(placements) AS total_placements
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.salary_by_major

datasets.name: ee25bc9e
queryLines: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT employer) AS active_employers
FROM `co-op_data`.default.salary_by_employer;

datasets.name: 4d834717
queryLines:
SELECT
  primary_major,
  avg_hourly_rate,
  placements
FROM `co-op_data`.default.salary_by_major
ORDER BY avg_hourly_rate DESC
LIMIT 10;

datasets.name: 4c32da21
queryLines:
SELECT
  year AS Year,
  avg_hourly_rate,
  placements
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.salary_by_year
WHERE
  array_contains(:selected_years, CAST(year AS INT))
ORDER BY
  year

datasets.name: bc94b4a4
queryLines:
SELECT
  state,
  avg_hourly_rate,
  placements
FROM `co-op_data`.default.salary_by_state
ORDER BY avg_hourly_rate DESC
LIMIT 10;

datasets.name: 5f2da126
queryLines:
SELECT
  job_source,
  placements,
  avg_hourly_rate
FROM `co-op_data`.default.salary_by_job_source
ORDER BY placements DESC
Limit 10;

SELECT
  employer,
  employer_industry,
  total_placements,
  avg_hourly_rate,
  rank_by_placements
FROM `co-op_data`.default.employer_rankings
WHERE rank_by_placements <= 10
ORDER BY rank_by_placements;

datasets.name: 210fd95e
queryLines:
WITH top_employers AS (
  SELECT
    employer
  FROM
    `co-op_data`.default.salary_by_employer
  WHERE
    placements >= 10
  ORDER BY
    placements DESC
  LIMIT 10
),
employer_yearly_trends AS (
  SELECT
    Year,
    Employer,
    COUNT(*) AS total_placements,
    ROUND(AVG(Hourly_Rate), 2) AS avg_hourly_rate
  FROM
    `co-op_data`.default.lindner_coop_cleaned_data
  WHERE
    Year >= 2020
    AND Employer IN (
      SELECT
        employer
      FROM
        top_employers
    )
    AND Hourly_Rate IS NOT NULL
    AND array_contains(:selected_years, CAST(Year AS INT))
  GROUP BY
    Year,
    Employer
)
SELECT
  *
FROM
  employer_yearly_trends
ORDER BY
  Employer,
  Year

datasets.name: 8bf4b941
8137e260
queryLines:
SELECT
  churn_status,
  COUNT(*) AS employer_count
FROM `co-op_data`.default.employer_churn_status
GROUP BY churn_status;

datasets.name: c65ac6cd
queryLines:
SELECT
  employer,
  last_active_year
FROM `co-op_data`.default.employer_churn_status
WHERE churn_status = 'Churned'
ORDER BY last_active_year;

datasets.name: 1f0ed753
queryLines:
SELECT
  Year,
  COUNT(DISTINCT Employer) AS active_employers
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.lindner_coop_cleaned_data
WHERE
  array_contains(:selected_years, CAST(Year AS INT))
GROUP BY
  Year
ORDER BY
  Year

datasets.name: c84fc96a
queryLines:
SELECT
  employer,
  (global_latest_year - last_active_year) AS years_inactive
FROM `co-op_data`.default.employer_churn_status
WHERE churn_status = 'Churned'
ORDER BY years_inactive DESC;

datasets.name: 55784e66
datasets.displayName: Top 10 High‑Value Employers Who Have Churned
queryLines:
WITH employer_history AS (
    SELECT
        Employer,
        COUNT(*) AS total_placements
    FROM `co-op_data`.default.lindner_coop_cleaned_data
    GROUP BY Employer
),
churned_employers AS (
    SELECT
        e.Employer AS employer,
        e.total_placements,
        c.last_active_year
    FROM employer_history e
    JOIN `co-op_data`.default.employer_churn_status c
        ON e.Employer = c.employer
    WHERE c.churn_status = 'Churned'
)
SELECT *
FROM churned_employers
ORDER BY total_placements DESC
LIMIT 10;

datasets.name: state_placements_map
queryLines:
SELECT
  CONCAT('US-', state) AS state_code,
  placements,
  avg_hourly_rate,
  state AS state_name
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.salary_by_state
ORDER BY
  placements DESC;

datasets.name: yoy_growth
queryLines:
WITH all_years AS (
  -- Get placement counts for ALL years first (before filtering)
  SELECT
    Year,
    COUNT(*) AS total_placements
  FROM
    `co-op_data`.default.lindner_coop_cleaned_data
  WHERE
    Year IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY
    Year
),
growth_calc AS (
  -- Calculate YoY growth based on consecutive calendar years
  SELECT
    Year,
    total_placements,
    LAG(total_placements) OVER (ORDER BY Year) AS prev_year_placements,
    CASE
      WHEN
        LAG(total_placements) OVER (ORDER BY Year) IS NOT NULL
        AND LAG(total_placements) OVER (ORDER BY Year) > 0
      THEN
        ROUND(
          (
            (total_placements - LAG(total_placements) OVER (ORDER BY Year))
              * 100.0
              / LAG(total_placements) OVER (ORDER BY Year)
          ),
          2
        )
      ELSE NULL
    END AS yoy_growth_rate_pct
  FROM
    all_years
)
-- Now apply the year filter for display
SELECT
  Year,
  total_placements,
  prev_year_placements,
  yoy_growth_rate_pct
FROM
  growth_calc
WHERE
  array_contains(:selected_years, CAST(Year AS INT))
ORDER BY
  Year

datasets.name: year_filter
queryLines:
SELECT DISTINCT
  Year
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.lindner_coop_cleaned_data
WHERE
  Year IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY
  Year DESC

datasets.name: employer_segments
queryLines:
SELECT
  Employer AS employer,
  Total_Hires AS placements,
  Avg_Salary AS avg_hourly_rate,
  Tier_Name AS segment
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.lcs_employer_segments
ORDER BY
  Total_Hires DESC

datasets.name: segment_summary
queryLines:
SELECT
  Tier_Name AS segment,
  COUNT(*) AS employer_count,
  ROUND(AVG(Total_Hires), 1) AS avg_placements,
  ROUND(AVG(Avg_Salary), 2) AS avg_hourly_rate,
  SUM(Total_Hires) AS total_placements
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.lcs_employer_segments
GROUP BY
  Tier_Name
ORDER BY
  Tier_Name

datasets.name: top_employers_by_segment
queryLines:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT
    Tier_Name AS segment,
    Employer AS employer,
    Total_Hires AS placements,
    Avg_Salary AS avg_hourly_rate,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
        PARTITION BY Tier_Name
        ORDER BY Total_Hires DESC, Avg_Salary DESC
      ) AS rank_in_segment
  FROM
    `co-op_data`.default.lcs_employer_segments
)
SELECT
  segment,
  employer,
  placements,
  avg_hourly_rate,
  rank_in_segment
FROM
  ranked
WHERE
  rank_in_segment <= 5
ORDER BY
  segment,
  rank_in_segment

datasets.name: cluster_quality
queryLines:
SELECT
  k,
  inertia
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.lcs_elbow_data
ORDER BY
  k

datasets.name: industry_by_segment
queryLines:
WITH top_industries AS (
  SELECT
    r.employer_industry AS industry,
    COUNT(*) AS total_employer_count
  FROM
    `co-op_data`.default.lcs_employer_segments s
      LEFT JOIN `co-op_data`.default.employer_rankings r
        ON s.Employer = r.employer
  WHERE
    r.employer_industry IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY
    r.employer_industry
  ORDER BY
    total_employer_count DESC
  LIMIT 10
)
SELECT
  s.Tier_Name AS segment,
  r.employer_industry AS industry,
  COUNT(*) AS employer_count,
  SUM(s.Total_Hires) AS total_placements
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.lcs_employer_segments s
    LEFT JOIN `co-op_data`.default.employer_rankings r
      ON s.Employer = r.employer
WHERE
  r.employer_industry IN (
    SELECT
      industry
    FROM
      top_industries
  )
GROUP BY
  s.Tier_Name,
  r.employer_industry
ORDER BY
  s.Tier_Name,
  employer_count DESC

datasets.name: salary_distribution_boxplot
queryLines:
SELECT
  Tier_Name AS segment,
  Avg_Salary AS avg_hourly_rate
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.lcs_employer_segments
ORDER BY
  Tier_Name

datasets.name: segment_kpis
queryLines:
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT Tier_Name) AS total_segments,
  COUNT(*) AS total_employers,
  ROUND(AVG(Avg_Salary), 2) AS overall_avg_rate,
  SUM(Total_Hires) AS total_placements
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.lcs_employer_segments

datasets.name: all_employers_by_segment
queryLines:
SELECT
  Tier_Name AS segment,
  Employer AS employer,
  Total_Hires AS placements,
  Avg_Salary AS avg_hourly_rate,
  Median_Salary AS median_hourly_rate
FROM
  `co-op_data`.default.lcs_employer_segments
ORDER BY
  Tier_Name,
  Total_Hires DESC

frame.title: Average Hourly Wage
frame.description:
The overall average hourly wage earned by students across all co‑op 
placements in the selected time period.

frame.title: Total Student Placements
frame.description:
The total number of co‑op placements recorded in the dataset 
reflects the overall program scale and activity.

frame.title: Active Employers
frame.description:
The number of distinct employers that have 
hired at least one student through LCS co‑op 
placements.

frame.title: Average Hourly Wage by Major
frame.description: Compares average hourly wages across academic majors to highlight differences in compensation outcomes by field of study.

frame.title: Bar 4
frame.description: Shows how average hourly wages have changed year over year, helping identify long‑term trends and shifts in compensation outcomes.

frame.title: Average Hourly Wage by State
frame.description: Compares average student wages across U.S. states to identify geographic differences in co‑op compensation.

frame.title: Placement Channels — Volume vs Pay
frame.description: Shows how different job placement channels compare based on the number of student placements and average hourly pay.

frame.title: Top Employers by Placement Volume
frame.description:
Lists employers with the highest number of student placements, helping LCS identify key employer partners and engagement 
opportunities.

lines: **LCS Co-op & Scholarship Analytics Dashboard** This dashboard provides an overview of LCS co‑op outcomes, including student placements, employer participation, wage trends, and geographic distribution. All insights are generated automatically from the most recent data to support consistent, data‑driven decision‑making.

frame.title: Employer Hiring Volume Trends (2020–Present)
frame.description: Shows how student hiring volume has increased or decreased over time by employer.

frame.title: Employer Average Pay Trends (2020–Present)
frame.description: Shows how average hourly pay has changed over time by employer.

frame.title: Churned Employers
frame.description:
Number of employers that have not hired any students in the past 
two academic years.

Shows the number of employers classified as Active or Churned.
An employer is considered churned if they have not hired any students in the past two academic years.
frame.title: Employer Churn Status Overview
Churned Employers
frame.description:
Lists employers that have not hired any students in the last two academic years, indicating disengagement 
from the co‑op program.

frame.title: Bar 14
frame.description: Tracks how the number of employers actively hiring students changes over time, providing context for employer disengagement trends.

frame.title: Student Placement Density by State
frame.description: Geographic visualization showing the number of co-op placements across U.S. states, highlighting regions with the highest student placement activity.

frame.title: Year-over-Year Placement Growth
frame.description:
Tracks annual placement volume (bars) and year-over-year growth percentage (line). The program experienced rapid expansion in 2021
 (peak: 3,341 placements, +245%), followed by a correction period (2022–2024) with negative growth rates as volume normalized.
Recovery began in 2025 (+13.4%). Note: 2026 data excluded (incomplete year).

frame.title: Top 10 High-Value Employers Who Have Churned
frame.description:
Shows employers that historically hired a large number of students but have not hired recently. 
These are high‑impact churn cases that may require immediate re‑engagement.

lines: **LCS Employer Segmentation Dashboard** This dashboard uses **K-Means clustering (K=4)** to segment LCS co-op employers based on two key dimensions: **total student placements (hiring volume)** and **average hourly rate (compensation level)**. The segmentation identifies four distinct employer tiers, **ordered by average placement volume**: * **Tier 0 — Anchor Partner**: Highest volume employers with consistent rates * **Tier 1 — Core Partners**: High volume employers with moderate compensation * **Tier 2 — Premium Partners**: Moderate volume with higher-paying, competitive compensation * **Tier 3 — Standard Partners**: Largest segment by employer count, lower volume per employer, moderate pay The dashboard enables **strategic employer relationship management** by visualizing segment characteristics, industry distribution, salary variance, and placement volume. Use these insights to prioritize engagement efforts, identify growth opportunities, and understand employer partner composition across the LCS co-op ecosystem.

frame.title: Employer Segmentation Map
frame.description:
Each point represents an employer, positioned by total placements (x-axis) and average hourly rate (y-axis). Colors 
indicate the assigned segment based on median thresholds.

frame.title: Employer Count by Segment
frame.description:
Number of employers in each segment, showing the distribution across high/lower volume and 
high/lower pay categories.

frame.title: Segment Summary
frame.description:
Key metrics for each employer segment: count of employers, average placements per employer, average hourly rate, 
and total student placements.

frame.title: Elbow Method — Optimal Number of Clusters
frame.description:
Shows inertia (within-cluster sum of squares) for different values of K (number of clusters). The "elbow" at K=4 indicates 
the optimal trade-off between model complexity and clustering quality, matching the gold layer analysis.

frame.title: Segment Share of Total Placements
frame.description:
Shows what percentage of total student placements each employer segment represents. Visualizes the 
relative contribution of each tier to overall placement volume.

frame.title: Industry Distribution by Segment
frame.description: Shows which industries are represented in each employer tier, with bar segments indicating employer count per industry.

frame.title: Salary Distribution by Segment
frame.description: Shows the spread and variance of average hourly rates within each employer segment. Box plot displays quartiles, median, min/max values to answer: How homogeneous are employers within each segment?

frame.title: Total Segments
frame.description:
Number of employer segments identified through K-Means 
clustering

frame.title: Total Employers
frame.description: Total number of employers included in the segmentation analysis

frame.title: Average Hourly Rate
frame.description: Overall average hourly rate across all employers in all segments

frame.title: Total Placements
frame.description: Total student placements across all segmented employers

frame.title: Complete Employer Directory by Segment
frame.description: Comprehensive list of all 282 employers with segment assignments, placement volumes, and compensation metrics. Sorted by segment tier and hiring volume.
